quote_of_day.text: >>20168 Caste kangers care only about caste because it's the only thing they have to kang about. You can be a foot taller and whiter than them and they'll still think they're superior there's no winning with these retards just ignore them and live your life.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of sacrilegious remarks moved into the board this morning, with Anon criticizing the concept of sacred animals. This sparked a debate that drew scattered showers of responses throughout the day.

A front of caste-based tensions developed in the afternoon, with one anon arguing that Dravidian identity was superior to others. This brought forth a squall of counter-arguments and insults, making for a choppy discourse climate.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of erotic asphyxiation fetish discussion occurred in the evening, accompanied by a cold snap of incel anxiety as one poster sought transformation into a 'Chad' within a short span.

Elsewhere, a light drizzle of Ambedkar-related chatter and a passing fog of gobarjeeta girl group discussion added to the complexity of the day's weather.

articles.title: Anon Criticizes Sacred Animal Concept, Sparking Debate
articles.body:
Ambedkar kicked off the discussion by calling the concept of a sacred animal 'the most retarded thing a culture could do.' They argued that eating beef is justified, saying 'it's just a fucking animal,' and suggested that those who consider it sacred should get their 'malnutritioned brain checked.' Anon responded by suggesting that the disagreement stems from different frameworks and moral layers.

They noted that even if one doesn't care about animal welfare, the spiritual layer of belief is a separate issue that can't be easily dismissed.

Another anon defended the idea of protecting certain animals, citing the concept of 'Aghniya' and the historical protection of cows in their original form.

They acknowledged that some cows were mixed with others and sold, but emphasized that this doesn't change the fact that they were meant to be protected.

The conversation also touched on genetics, with one anon disputing the claim that the Y chromosome is weaker than the X. They explained that the Y chromosome serves a specific purpose and can't be compared directly to the X chromosome.

Narthie later expressed frustration with the thread, saying that it was initially decent but got ruined by a few users, including the OP, who they called 'retards.' Anon discussed the idea of starting a new branch of a faith, with one participant suggesting that they could create a new branch of the 'dharmic tree' that incorporates their own beliefs, including the consumption of beef.

They noted that they couldn't abandon their spiritual experiences and that their life would be tied to dharmic beliefs.

The discussion also involved dietary choices, with one anon mentioning their aim to recreate the Indus Valley Diet, which includes meat and seafood, along with low-glycemic index carbs and fermented foods.

Other topics included the comparison of different faiths and their symbols, with one anon noting that any symbol of faith can be bastardized if its followers don't have the power to defend it.

The thread also included several exchanges that were more confrontational in nature, with some users engaging in personal attacks and using derogatory language.

articles.title: Caste Dynamics and Regional Identity
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion by asserting a connection between South Indian temple creation, mathematical art, and Dravidian genetics, while criticizing a supposed dalit from Tamil Nadu for claiming a history that Anon believes was stolen by "dasyu" people.

Another anon responded by questioning the OP's claims about caste and regional identity, stating that certain communities in the South are not Sanskritized dominant castes but rather mercantile and elite castes that historically patronized Brahmins.

This anon clarified that their comments were strictly between Dwijas and that they did not concern themselves with other castes.

A respondent argued that the OP's views on genetics and caste were misguided, stating that all Indians are on a genetic cline and that plotting Brahmins on this cline would show overlap with other castes, including Dalits.

This anon also criticized the OP for not considering autosomal studies.

Anon also debated the concept of "UC" (upper caste) status and its relationship to Dwija status, with one arguing that they are not the same thing and that Pasmanda can be a UC too.

Another anon noted that regionalism in India is independent of Dalits and that most UCs are not interested in pan-UC identity.

The discussion also touched on the history of Brahmins and their role in Indian society, with one anon stating that Brahmins have historically been a middle-class bunch due to their focus on academia and bureaucracy.

A respondent countered that this view is simplistic and childish, and that the understanding of anthropology is more complex.

Throughout the discussion, various anons exchanged views on caste, genetics, and regional identity, often using strong language and personal attacks.

articles.title: Erotic Asphyxiation Fetish Discussed
articles.body:
Anon shared several cases of individuals who died from erotic asphyxiation, a fetish that involves strangulation or hanging to induce arousal.

One case involved a 24-year-old Onlyfans and PornHub erotic gay model who died during a livestream in Italy, where he was discussing the COVID-19 pandemic.

Ambedkar provided details on various incidents, including a 23-year-old woman who died while engaging in an erotic asphyxiation session with her partner. The partner had waited too long to intervene, leading to the woman's death from a heart attack.

Another case involved an 18-year-old man who streamed his own death on an erotic platform, using a bed sheet as a noose.

Some posters discussed the possible reasons behind this fetish, with Ambedkar suggesting that it may be linked to brain deprivation of blood, creating a hallucinogenic effect that leads to powerful orgasms.

Others noted that people may become addicted to this fetish like a drug addiction.

One poster, Fuwawa, expressed curiosity about how individuals discover their affinity for erotic asphyxiation, while another, Gura, shared a personal experience of being unaffected by gore but finding hanging videos uncomfortable to watch.

The thread also included graphic descriptions of various incidents, including a case of a 27-year-old man who died while streaming on LiveJasmin, wearing a condom. His stream continued for 23 minutes before admins were notified of his death.

The discussion also touched on the topic of desensitization, with Fuwawa noting that individual reactions to such content can vary greatly.

articles.title: Ambedkar discusses gobarjeeta girl group
articles.body:
Ambedkar started a discussion about an upcoming girl group called gobarjeeta kumari. They wondered if readers' sisters were auditioning and expressed interest in seeing the group perform. Respondents reacted with diverse comments.

Dyaush and Anon expressed support for the group and appreciation for their appearance. Lungi and Anon made derogatory comments about the appearance of a specific individual.

Some respondents, including Mococo and Haggu, made explicit and misogynistic remarks about female idols and their potential personal lives. Others, like Ozymandias, questioned the implications of a young idol group.

articles.title: Incel Seeks Transformation into Chad in 2 Months
articles.body:
Narthie asked how to transform from incel to Chad in two months. Anon Kantabai Ownes responded with blunt advice: go to the gym, eat protein, sleep, grow a beard, get a haircut, stop masturbating, and stop complaining.

Nepajeet noted that changing personality is hard, especially if one is autistic. Lungi suggested that Narthie stop using labels like 'randi' and instead think of women as simply promiscuous.

Fuwawa recommended an extremely high-carb diet with at least 200 grams of simple carbs daily, along with exercise.
